scenarios.description: Two sequential steps connected by an AFTER_SUCCESS_REFERENCE dependency.
A root step fans out to two parallel steps via PARALLEL_REFERENCE dependencies.
A step joins on two predecessors via JOIN_REFERENCE dependencies.
An audit step triggers only after a failure via AFTER_FAILURE_REFERENCE.
A workflow with one step per declared step type, dual approval, exponential retry, long timeout and rollback compensation.
A step overrides the workflow default retry with FIXED_REFERENCE.
A human approval step overrides the workflow default retry with MANUAL_REFERENCE.
A step overrides the workflow default timeout with SHORT_REFERENCE.
A human approval step overrides the workflow default timeout with MANUAL_REFERENCE.
A step overrides the workflow default compensation with MANUAL_COMPENSATION_REFERENCE.
A human approval step overrides the workflow default compensation with HUMAN_COMPENSATION_REFERENCE.
A workflow whose approval policy is USER_REFERENCE.
A workflow whose approval policy is SUPERVISOR_REFERENCE.
A workflow whose approval policy is ADMIN_REFERENCE.
A workflow whose entry condition is SWITCH_REFERENCE and exit condition is ELSE_REFERENCE.
A workflow whose exit condition is NEVER_REFERENCE.
A workflow containing an optional notification step with no workflow-level timeout.
The approval policy reference is bound to a different tenant than the workflow.
The timeout reference is bound to a different organization than the workflow.
